target.title: fix: plugins update rewrites config for install-record-only updates
target.body:
Fixes #98419

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users following `gateway status --deep` plugin drift hints after a core upgrade could have `openclaw plugins update <exact official npm spec>` fail at the config write step even though the only durable change was an installed-plugin index record. On a Windows 2026.6.11 upgrade, updating `@openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11` downloaded the package but then tried to replace a larger user-authored `openclaw.json` with a smaller projected config, triggering the size-drop guard and leaving official plugin drift unresolved.

This PR is scoped to install-record-only plugin updates. It does not change legacy `plugins.installs` cleanup, hook updates, or plugin id migration paths that genuinely mutate authored config.

## Why This Change Was Made

`plugins update` now compares the projected next source config with the mutation-start source config after plugin install records have been stripped. If the source config is unchanged, the command commits only the persisted plugin install index and skips `replaceConfigFile(...)` entirely. Existing config-writing behavior remains in place when legacy plugin install records need to be removed from config or when hook/config state actually changes.

The records-only path now preserves the same freshness barrier that the config-writing path had: before the records-only commit is allowed to complete, it rereads the writable config snapshot and rejects stale root config hashes, config path changes, included-file target changes, and included-file hash changes. The check runs through the existing install-record commit rollback path, so a stale config race restores the previous install index instead of refreshing plugin registry metadata from the mutation-start config.

## User Impact

Operators can repair official plugin version drift after upgrading OpenClaw without rewriting or shrinking their existing `openclaw.json` when no config mutation is needed. Config size-drop protection still protects real risky writes, and concurrent config edits still fail closed instead of leaving plugin index metadata derived from stale config.

## Evidence

Real before evidence from a Windows 11 upgrade to `openclaw@2026.6.11`:

```shell
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.11 (e085fa1)

$ openclaw gateway status --deep
CLI version: 2026.6.11
Gateway version: 2026.6.11
Connectivity probe: ok
Plugin version drift: 4 active official plugins not on gateway 2026.6.11
- brave: 2026.6.11-beta.2 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.11
- discord: 2026.6.11-beta.2 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.11
- qqbot: 2026.6.11-beta.2 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.11
- whatsapp: 2026.6.11-beta.2 (npm) -> expected 2026.6.11

$ openclaw plugins update @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11
Installed @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11 into <managed npm project>
Config write rejected: C:\Users\Administrator\.openclaw\openclaw.json (size-drop:40696->14568). Rejected payload saved to <redacted rejected path>.
[openclaw] Could not start the CLI.
```

After-fix real CLI proof from this branch using an isolated temporary Windows home/state/config, not the production user config:

```shell
$ node --import tsx src/entry.ts plugins install npm:@openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11-beta.2
Installed plugin: brave
Restart the gateway to load plugins.

$ sha256sum <isolated OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH>
ED9A9930DD5B22E1E0AC549C57DF76DB0F8DDF1C6F88EA392B0206EC73825ED1  openclaw.json
# size: 199 bytes

$ node --import tsx src/entry.ts plugins update @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11
Installing @openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11 into <isolated OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR>\npm\projects\openclaw-brave-plugin-...
Linked peerDependency "openclaw" -> <source checkout>
Plugin manifest id "brave" differs from npm package name "@openclaw/brave-plugin"; using manifest id as the config key.
Updated brave: 2026.6.11-beta.2 -> 2026.6.11.
Restart the gateway to load plugins and hooks.

$ sha256sum <isolated OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH>
ED9A9930DD5B22E1E0AC549C57DF76DB0F8DDF1C6F88EA392B0206EC73825ED1  openclaw.json
# size: 199 bytes

$ node -e '<read persisted plugin install index>'
{
  "brave": {
    "source": "npm",
    "spec": "@openclaw/brave-plugin@2026.6.11",
    "resolvedName": "@openclaw/brave-plugin",
    "resolvedVersion": "2026.6.11"
  }
}
```

Observed after-fix result: the real update command exited 0, updated the persisted `brave` install record from `2026.6.11-beta.2` to `2026.6.11`, and left the authored config hash and byte size unchanged.

Source-level proof added in this PR:

- Added a regression test where a full source config has persisted plugin install records, `plugins update brave` changes only the install record, and the command:
  - calls `writePersistedInstalledPluginIndexInstallRecords(...)` with the updated record,
  - does not call `replaceConfigFile(...)` / `writeConfigFile(...)`,
  - refreshes the plugin registry with the unchanged source config.
- Added root-config freshness coverage: if the root config hash changes between mutation-start and records-only commit, the command rejects the update, restores the previous install records, and does not refresh the plugin registry.
- Added included-config freshness coverage: if an included config file hash changes between mutation-start and records-only commit, the command rejects the update, restores the previous install records, and does not refresh the plugin registry.
- Existing legacy-record cleanup tests still expect config writes when `plugins.installs` must be removed from authored config.

Validation run on this branch:

```shell
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts --testNamePattern="plugins cli update"
# 1 file passed, 36 tests passed

git diff --check
# passed

node_modules\.bin\oxlint.cmd src/cli/plugins-update-command.ts src/cli/plugins-install-record-commit.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.update.test.ts
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-plugin-update-sizedrop.tsbuildinfo
# passed

python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local
# autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
```
target.author: ooiuuii
